content:
Corrent me if I am wrong.

To my understanding, rusttest is running in userspace, and be managed by Cargo. Based on this, if one test contains functions that are solely to the Kernel, e.g kmalloc(), the building of this test will fail and report error.
This topic was moved here from #General > Rust fork? by Miguel Ojeda

Douglas Su said:

To my understanding, rusttest is running in userspace, and be managed by Cargo.

No, it is not managed by Cargo. We use Cargo to build a sysroot with the custom alloc crate because it is the "official" way of building std etc. But the actual compiling and running of the tests happens with Kbuild as usual.
This means in the future, if we manage to drop the std dependency, we can drop building the sysroot too.
(e.g. to run them in kernel space)

Douglas Su said:

Based on this, if one test contains functions that are solely to the Kernel, e.g kmalloc(), the building of this test will fail and report error.

This is correct.
